text.title: eu-ai-act-compliance Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end example: high-risk credit-scoring agent compliance package.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: A fintech deploys an LLM-powered credit-scoring agent in DE, FR, NL. The agent ingests applicant text, retrieves bureau data, and outputs an approve/reject decision with a score. The applicant is informed the decision is automated.

example.title: Classification (step 1)
Article 13 disclosure copy (step 4)
example.p: "This decision was produced by an automated system. You may request human review under Article 22 GDPR. The system was tested for fairness on protected attributes and a model card is available on request."

example.title: Reviewer flag (step 5)
example.p: Reviewer subagent flagged a draft that cited "Article 47 high-risk obligations" — no such article in this Act. Flag returned to docgen, which corrected to Article 11 (technical documentation) + Article 13 (transparency).

section.title: Outcome
section.p: System registered in EU AI database with reg-id `EU-AI-2026-07-DE-0014`. Post-market monitoring dashboard shows monthly bias deltas; on next feature ship (model upgrade to v2.0), CI auto-triggers step 1 re-classification.
